element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Safe hub where Will recovers health, switches forms, and gains abilities
Establishes narrative frame: comet approaching, Will chosen as savior
Primary playable character; techniques focus on area access and exploration
Straightforward melee system; health bars display as red spheres; no equipment management
Defeat all enemies in room to receive permanent stat increases (attack/defense/health); replaces traditional XP
Dark knight form with enhanced combat damage and reach; more damage than Will
Energy manifestation form; highest damage output; joins party later in game
Based on real-world locations (Incan ruins, Nazca Lines, Angkor Wat, Great Wall, Egyptian pyramids); each holds puzzle pieces
Global artifact collection quest; essential for Tower of Babel finale
Final location where Will and Kara reveal dual-knight nature and confront Dark Gaia
Optional collectible; missable if not found before story advances; only side quest

typical_relief_triggers: Clearing all enemies in a room
typical_tension_triggers: Permanent ability loss if missing red jewels
Fixed difficulty setting with no adjustment
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Progressive overworld exploration interspersed with fixed-difficulty dungeon challenges; tension released through stat progression system and save point access; minimal ability to retreat (no backtracking except late game)

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Red Jewels hidden in areas; collectible-gating for completionists

difficulty_curve.early_game: Introduction to melee combat and room-clearing jewel system; basic enemy encounters establish baseline; Will's exploration-focused abilities introduce puzzle-solving
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Multiple character forms available (Freedan, Shadow); dungeon complexity increases with real-world ruin theming; Mystic Statue collection across globe introduces varied environments
difficulty_curve.late_game: Character transformation abilities unlock increasingly complex puzzle access; Tower of Babel serves as climactic endgame; final confrontation with Dark Gaia as comet manifestation
notable_spikes: First transformation into Freedan (combat power spike)
Tower of Babel arrival (complexity and enemy density increase)

notable_level_patterns.description: Dungeons themed after actual historical sites (Great Pyramid, Great Wall of China, Angkor Wat, Incan ruins, Nazca Lines) that serve dual narrative/gameplay purpose: advance story by collecting Mystic Statues while solving environment-specific puzzles requiring specific character forms

Non-combat safe zones accessible throughout game serving as save points, health recovery, form-switching hubs, and ability-gain locations; features visual representation of Will's alter egos (Freedan, Shadow statues)

Combat encounters structured as discrete rooms where defeating all enemies grants permanent stat jewels (attack/defense/health); encourages tactical clearing rather than avoidance; enemies respawn if area revisited but bonuses don't stack

Certain areas inaccessible without specific character form; Will's techniques unlock exploration paths, Freedan's provide combat utility, Shadow unlocks late-game content; creates replay-adjacent gating within linear progression
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Various dungeons requiring Freedan or Shadow
